rationale: Decision 1306ca6a explicitly forbids hard-coding personal values and requires all assumptions to be configuration/data so the app is general-purpose. The Excel's flat 42.6% tax rate and hard-coded SS income are exactly the kind of hard-coded personal constants that 1306ca6a prohibits. Decision 2949f831 reinforces this by defining a Scenario as a named, configurable assumption set that includes 'tax, income streams, contributions' as explicit fields — meaning they must be parameterised from the start, not inherited as Excel constants. Replicating those Excel simplifications in v1 would directly violate both binding decisions, leaving 'improve out of the gate' as the only consistent choice.
